name: James
review_text: Honestly the smoothest hiring experience I've had in Korea. Scarlett actually read my CV before our call lol

name: Oliver
review_text:
I went through three other recruiters before finding Bridge and the difference was immediate. Other places felt like a factory ~ sign here, good luck, bye. Bridge actually sat down with me and asked about my teaching style, my deal breakers, everything. Night and day.

The school they matched me with has been brilliant. Co teachers are kind, hours are reasonable, housing is way nicer than I expected. Six months in and I'm already thinking about renewing.

If you're on the fence just send them a message. They reply fast and they don't push you into anything that doesn't fit.

name: Aoife
review_text: Bridge made my move to Korea feel almost easy~ they answered every silly question I had at 2am my time!! Cannot recommend enough.

name: Thando
review_text: Super professional. Got me a job within 3 weeks of applying.

name: Hayden
review_text:
Was already working in Korea and looking for a new company when I came across Bridge. My time at the previous job was wrapping up and Bridge not only matched me with an academy that has the perfect work life balance ~ they introduced me to the loveliest team I've met in this country. Genuinely.

Scarlett took the time to actually understand what I wanted out of the next role. We talked about commute, class size, the vibe of the director. She didn't just throw postings at me. When the offer came through it was already a good fit before I even visited the school.

A year later I'm still here, still happy. The school still asks Bridge for new hires whenever they need someone. That tells you everything.

name: Marcus
review_text: Quick, clean, no nonsense. Exactly what I needed.

name: Emma
review_text:
Bridge has been a huge help finding a new school. They really listened to the kind of place I was looking for. Housing in particular was important to me and they made sure that was sorted before I committed to anything :)

Every step my recruiter helped me out. Even now after starting they still check in. Highly recommend!

name: Connor
review_text: Bridge is an excellent recruiter. They listened to all of my preferences and connected me to job opportunities that worked for me. They were friendly and easy to talk to throughout the entire process.

name: Lerato
review_text: Best decision I made all year!!

name: Brittany
review_text:
I'd been looking at jobs in Korea for ages and was super overwhelmed. So many recruiters, so many red flags. Bridge was the only one that didn't feel sketchy.

They were upfront about salary ranges, didn't promise things they couldn't deliver, and walked me through every step including the visa stuff which honestly stressed me out the most. The apostille process was a lot but they had a checklist ready so I just followed it.

Now I've been teaching at a lovely kindy in Seoul for 8 months. The kids are sweet, my director is great, and I actually have a life outside of work which I never managed in the US lol.

If you're nervous about making the jump ~ just talk to them. No pressure, just real info.

name: Liam
review_text: Solid team. Knew what they were doing.

name: Niamh
review_text: Loved working with Bridge! Felt like talking to a friend who happened to know everything about teaching in Korea :)

name: Jess
review_text: I had a really tight timeline because of my previous contract ending and Bridge somehow made it work. Three weeks from first email to flight booked.

name: Pieter
review_text: Recommended by a friend who used Bridge two years ago. He was right ~ they're the real deal!

name: Sarah
review_text:
From the first video interview onwards every interaction with Bridge was friendly, professional, and helpful. They went to the effort to find out exactly what my requirements were and matched me with a really good fit.

It's such a good match that I'm already negotiating to extend my contract by another year. Scarlett always answered my queries, no matter how minor. And when she didn't know the answer she'd find out and get back to me quickly.

This carried on even after I arrived. A lot of agents consider the job done once you land but Bridge is still around for questions or just a friendly chat.

I heartily recommend anyone considering teaching in Korea to talk to Bridge first :)

name: Harry
review_text: Cheers Bridge! Top job all round.

name: Olivia
review_text: Honestly the best part was how transparent they were about everything. Salary, housing, school culture ~ no surprises after I arrived. I knew exactly what I was walking into.

name: Cian
review_text: Great experience, would use again!!

name: Zoe
review_text:
Bridge worked with me on my current role and was brilliant at every stage. They ensured I was kept informed every step of the way and gave me all the info I needed to be successful.

I'd like to thank them for being so supportive and encouraging, especially in the moments of uncertainty. They never mentioned any of the 'unlucky background matters' some other agents do. Highly recommend Bridge if you want a placement that actually matches your expectations.

name: Maia
review_text: Felt heard. That's the simplest way I can put it.

name: Tyler
review_text: Got me in a great hagwon in Busan. Director is awesome, students are mostly chill. Pay is on time every month. What else can you ask for honestly :)

name: Jack
review_text:
I have found Bridge to be very helpful and professional throughout the whole recruitment process. They helped find me the perfect position in a great company tailored to my skill set.

Moving to another country presented many challenges and questions but Bridge was always available and offered sound advice. Having their help throughout took away a lot of the stress and I'm very grateful.

name: Chloe
review_text: 10 out of 10!!

name: Megan
review_text: Just wanted to drop a note ~ been at my school for two months now and everything is exactly as Bridge described it. Such a refreshing change from horror stories I've read on Reddit.
